summary: Implementation contract for task-002 reviewed. All 7 ACs map cleanly to 7 demo_steps (AC1->mission/references grep; AC2->7 SKILL.md grep; AC3->2 agent files grep; AC4->git diff JSON byte-identity; AC5->before/after count of exceptions; AC6->single-quoted EOF grep; AC7->repo-wide zero-state verification). Non-goals correctly preserve the four allowed exceptions: event log --data, context write --data, packet create --file, packet create --content. Edge cases explicitly cover multi-line JSON (single-quoted heredoc), embedded single quotes (heredoc removes outer-quote escape problem), and fenced code block indentation (prefers same-indent heredoc or here-string fallback). Bash() wrapper and Agent() prompt-string embedding edge cases are also addressed. Planned actions enumerate a precise 20-hit baseline with per-file line numbers, giving high traceability. qa-engineer.md line 99 prose hint handled as a documentation-accuracy fix, not a command rewrite - correctly distinguished.
concerns: [low] AC1 wording: AC1 text says '4 files ... actually 5~6' which is imprecise - planned_actions correctly enumerates 6 files (specifying, polishing, pipeline, orchestration, building, evolving). Non-blocking prose ambiguity; worker has disambiguated in plan.

[low] AC6 grep pattern: Demo step for AC6 uses a heredoc grep pattern that may false-positive on prose mentioning <<EOF. Acceptable because scope is limited to files edited in this task; git diff inspection is the real gate.
[low] AC5 count parity: Demo step AC5 requires before/after counts of exception flags to be equal. Worker must record both counts in evidence as called out in the demo step text.
